notes: BFS crawl of all internal .html pages reachable from the homepage and known seed URLs (57 unique pages; /index.html treated as duplicate of homepage). No fetch failures. Site banner on whats-on.html states the website is under construction and content is static pending a new site.

hive_profile.name: The Hive, Worcester
hive_profile.partnership: Joint venture of the University of Worcester and Worcestershire County Council - 'The Hive is the first joint public and university library in Europe' (services.html, using-the-library.html). Built with support of Worcester City Council and Advantage West Midlands; opened to the public 2 July 2012 and officially opened by Her Majesty the Queen on 11 July 2012 (creating-the-hive.html).
hive_profile.address: The Hive, Sawmill Walk, The Butts, Worcester, WR1 3PD (getting-here.html, contact-us.html)

phone.general_enquiries: 01905 822866 - The Hive at the Worcestershire County Council Customer Services, 9am-5pm Monday-Friday (contact-us.html)
phone.site_footer: 01905 855000 - University of Worcester, Henwick Grove, WR2 6AJ (site-wide footer)

opening_hours.building: The Hive is open from 8:30am - 10pm, Monday to Sunday (opening-hours.html; plan-your-visit.html states 'Monday to Sunday 8.30am - 10.00 pm')
opening_hours.archives: Explore The Past / Archives and Archaeology opening times are published on the dedicated Explore The Past website explorethepast.co.uk (opening-hours.html, explore-the-past.html)
opening_hours.bank_holidays_2025: Good Friday 18 April Closed; Easter Saturday 19 April normal opening hours; Easter Sunday 20 April Closed; Easter Monday 21 April Closed; Early May Bank Holiday Monday 5 May reduced opening hours; Spring Bank Holiday Monday 26 May Closed; Summer Bank Holiday Monday 25 August Closed (opening-hours.html)

key_facts: Opened July 2012; first of its kind in Europe (facts-and-figures.html)
Contains a quarter of a million books (facts-and-figures.html)
10,000m2 of public space, over five floors (facts-and-figures.html)
Over 26,000 records of historic monuments and buildings (facts-and-figures.html)
Anticipating over a million visitors per year; over a million books issued in the first year (facts-and-figures.html)

extended_capabilities.capability: First joint public and university library in Europe
extended_capabilities.detail: Community members and university students get the same user experience; no segregation - university stock shelved alongside public stock; staff from both university and council work throughout the library; all users access the same services.
Open 8:30am - 10pm, Monday to Sunday (every day of the week).

extended_capabilities.capability: Explore the Past - Worcestershire Archive & Archaeology Service (Level 2)
extended_capabilities.detail: Home to the Worcestershire Archive and Archaeology Service; thousands of archaeological sites, historic buildings and landscapes recorded; 12 miles of original archives collected and preserved. Offers: area for consulting original archives on level 2; guidance from knowledgeable staff; facility to talk to archaeologists and access the archaeological record; large self-service area for microform and internet sources; local studies and archaeology library; a conservation room for the repair and preservation of documents; prearranged 'behind the scenes' group visits; programme of history and archaeology events for all ages. Original-document viewings (e.g. Stuart Collection) take place in the original documents examination room within 'access to original archives' opening hours.

Searchable database with digital mapping of all known archaeological sites, historic buildings and landscapes in the county; online search via Heritage Gateway; for detailed enquiries you can book to see an HER officer at The Hive.

Library catalogue search (quarter of a million titles plus all County branches, via wcc.ent.sirsidynix.net.uk), Archaeology report search (Heritage Gateway) and Archives search (CalmView at e-services.worcestershire.gov.uk).

The Business Centre at The Hive is home to BIPC Worcestershire, providing free and accessible business advice, support, information and resources, plus well-appointed meeting rooms with the latest technology. Free business databases COBRA (Complete Business Reference Adviser), Statista and FAME are accessible from any public computer in The Hive; start-up support, Intellectual Property information and funding signposted via the BIPC Worcestershire website.

Dedicated space for young people aged 15-24, open Monday-Friday 9am-4pm, with careers advisors providing 1-1 advice, CV writing, job search, interview preparation, apprenticeships/training routes, plus finance and health & wellbeing services. Careers Helpline 0300 666 3666.

extended_capabilities.capability: 800+ study spaces and bookable study spaces for students
extended_capabilities.detail: Over 800 study spaces across The Hive available to use for study; Level 0 can be used for group study; Level 4 is the silent study floor; 250 new computers bookable in advance (students page).

extended_capabilities.capability: Room hire / Space for hire with online booking and video conferencing
extended_capabilities.detail: From small huddle rooms to large function rooms at competitive prices; online booking system (librarybookings.worcestershire.gov.uk) with 360-degree virtual tours; new video-conferencing technology in many meeting rooms; concessionary rates for not-for-profit organisations; minimum one-hour charge with additional half hours; all prices include public liability insurance; University of Worcester members can book Hive rooms free for specific purposes; exhibition space also available for hire.

The Hive has its own onsite catering company (also manages the cafe) offering hospitality menus - buffets, platters, canapes, business breakfasts - for meetings, corporate training and conferences; special diets catered for; minimum 2 working days' notice.

Open Monday-Sunday 8.30am-9pm; hot and cold snacks, light meals, deli bar with made-to-order baguettes/sandwiches/salads; 10% student discount; five-star hygiene rating; fully accessible; Urban Eat ranges including vegan, vegetarian, gluten free and halal options.

extended_capabilities.capability: Children's library - one of the largest in the country (Level 1)
extended_capabilities.detail: Designed for children to find their own level; Story Pit recessed seating area for readings, performances and group work; access to 'Story Island', a unique outdoor teaching space for children accessed through the children's library; children-only PCs and printer/copier.

Free walk-in access inside the building for all library members to ~37 databases including Ancestry Library Edition, JSTOR, Academic Search Complete, Business Source Complete, CINAHL Complete, BMJ Journals, Oxford English Dictionary, Dictionary of National Biography, Times Digital Archive, Gale British newspapers, House of Commons Parliamentary Papers, Cambridge/Oxford/SAGE/Taylor & Francis journals, ScienceDirect, Project Muse, Drama Online and more.

The Stuart Collection (over one-thousand books on Stuart-period Britain 1603-1714, bequeathed by John Grainger in 1900, many first/last surviving copies); the Palfrey Collection (antiquarian studies and first editions key to Worcestershire study); the Stanbrook Abbey Press Collection (closed access on level 4); extensive Local Studies and Archaeology libraries.

extended_capabilities.capability: Research collections for family and local history
extended_capabilities.detail: Maps and plans (historic Ordnance Survey, tithe, inclosure and estate plans, many digital); census returns, International Genealogical Index, GRO Index of Births Marriages and Deaths, local newspapers, wills and probate; Worcestershire Photographic Survey and aerial photographic collections; archives of local families/businesses including the Earls of Coventry of Croome; Quarter Sessions, County and District Council records and the Worcester City Archive; Diocese of Worcester, parish and Non-conformist records.

Kaleidoscope by Robert Orchardson (aluminium, 2012, visible from all three main floors); Rack Alley by Clare Woods (600x500cm enamel and oil on aluminium, 2012, commissioned for the atrium); two John Clare-inspired paintings by James Fisher (2008).

eBook and eAudiobook service free to all library members 24/7 via library.bolindadigital.com/worcestershire or the BorrowBox app; borrow up to six eBooks and six eAudiobooks at a time; loans auto-expire with no late fees; syncs across up to six devices; requires library card number and PIN.

More than 8,000 students hosted since 2012. Free 'Package 1' visits (library tour plus activities such as Storytime, Book Blitz, Coding with Scratch, research skills, referencing) for Early Years through Sixth Form; charged 'Package 2' Explore the Past history/archive/archaeology workshops from a minimum of £40 per hour/class (individual WAAS workshops around £80, 90-120 minutes, e.g. Romans in Worcester, Anglo-Saxons, Tudor Worcester, Civil War, Victorian public health, WWI, WWII archaeology); webinars available; classroom resource packs £20 each; teacher training days hosted.

extended_capabilities.capability: Archaeology planning advice for developers (professional service)
extended_capabilities.detail: The Archive and Archaeology Service provides advice to landowners, developers and consultants on archaeological implications and opportunities of development in the County; advice on management and conservation of the rural and urban historic environment; workshops and training on heritage topics; plus professional services including digitisation, document conservation and a wide range of professional archaeological services.
Local history research groups, talks, workshops, tours, school activities, community excavations, archive film screenings, family history fairs, guided walks and a geology trail around The Hive.

extended_capabilities.capability: IT, gaming and printing on every level
extended_capabilities.detail: Public access PCs with internet and Microsoft Office on Levels 0-4; group study screen with 46-inch HDMI display (L0); two PS4 gaming zones (L0); high-speed printers/copiers on every level with credit-card top-up kiosks (cash kiosk only at the L1 Hub); A4 scanners on request (L3); privacy filters available (L1 Hub); dedicated catalogue search stations.

extended_capabilities.capability: The Studio theatre, exhibition spaces and Worcestershire Hub (Level 1)
extended_capabilities.detail: Level 1 'Discover' holds the children's library, the cafe, exhibition spaces and The Studio theatre as well as The Worcestershire Hub (county council customer services) and quick choice books; Level 2 also has meeting rooms and a Business Centre with a suite of three rooms, informal networking space and 24-hour newsfeed; secure archives on Level 0 with occasional special tours.

Fully accessible to wheelchairs, mobility scooters and pushchairs; lifts to all five public floors with Braille signage; Meet & Greet service bookable in advance; personal care suite on Level 1 with shower and lifting facilities (access card from staff); height-adjustable workstations and archive/microfilm tables; automated microfilm machine with foot pedal; induction loops in Meeting rooms 2, 3 and The Studio; 21 blue badge bays free for 3 hours in Cattlemarket car park; pre-arranged close access via Level 0; DisabledGo accessibility guide.
Rooms within The Hive can be used by customers to pray - ask a member of staff to be directed to an available room; Worcestershire County Council policy is to be equally supportive of all faiths.

Biomass boilers, River Severn river-water cooling, rainwater harvesting for WC flushing and archaeological washing, natural ventilation, water meadows for flood attenuation and 4000m2+ of biodiversity landscape; winner of 20+ awards including BREEAM Outstanding (2012), RIBA National Award (2013), SCONUL Library Design Award (2016) and ARA Record Keeping Service of the Year (2017); distinctive golden honeycomb cladding by Feilden Clegg Bradley Studios.

Instant free digital membership online (eBooks, eAudio, eMagazines and online resources) upgradeable to full membership with proof of name and address; UW students/staff use their university ID as their library card; dedicated Children's library card (designed by Liz Pichon) and Young Person's library card for ages 13-19; instant membership for ages 8-16 allows borrowing 2 books without a parent signature; full members get free computer/Wi-Fi access, DVD/Blu-ray hire, library activities and adult and family learning courses.

offerings: The Hive is home to a huge range of resources, including books, archives, DVDs, art collections and more

pages.title: Borrowing & reservations
pages.summary: Detailed borrowing entitlements and reservation rules for the joint Council/University library: item limits, University Short Loan (orange band) and Core text (blue band) rules, 80p reservations, last useful date, non-reservable items and account access. Includes a legacy COVID notice extending loans to 12 April 2021.
offerings: As a joint Council/University venture, The Hive and its rich variety of resources are accessible to everyone
All books, DVD's, CD's and other resources including specialised academic texts, are available to students and the public
Borrowing limits: Books 12; Sound recordings 12; Spoken word 12; DVDs 6; Blu-ray DVDs 3; Video 6; CDs 6; PlayStation games 4
University Short Loans (orange bands): community users can consult in the library only; University students can sign out for 2 days

Core University student texts (blue bands): community users may sign out one at a time (not requestable to other branches); University students may sign out 12 for two weeks; never reservable if available on the shelf at The Hive
All remaining books can be signed out for three weeks, 12 at a time
For 80p you can reserve an item for collection at a branch of your choice
Reservations typically satisfied in a few days but can be up to three weeks; reserved items held for approximately two weeks for collection
Last Useful Date: reservations auto-delete 12 months after being placed unless you notify the library
Non-reservable items: Reference, Short Loan, Periodicals, Special Collections
Access the online catalogue and your account at wcc.ent.sirsidynix.net.uk
COVID NOTICE - The return date for all existing loans has been extended until 12 April 2021. No fines or reservation fees will be applied during this time
what_you_need: Log in to your library account to reserve items
80p reservation fee per item

pages.title: How to get a library card
pages.summary: Joining the library: instant online Digital Membership (eBooks, eAudio, eMagazines and online resources) upgradeable to Full Library Membership in person with proof of name and address; UW students and staff use their university ID card; lists everything full membership unlocks.
offerings: Instantly become a digital member and get access to all of our services online
Digital Membership: access free eBook, eAudio and eMagazine collections and a wealth of other online resources; reserve a book to pick up from the library of your choice
Full Library Membership: borrow and reserve books, spoken word CDs and sound recordings from any library in the county
Borrow from our free collection of eBooks, eAudio and eMagazines, as well as our large Online Resource and Reference collections
Access library computers or Wi-Fi for free
DVD and Blu-Ray hire
Access library activities, such as Readers' Group, children's activities, Summer Reading Challenge and more
Adult and family learning courses both for qualifications and leisure
University of Worcester students and staff do not need to apply for a public library card; use your usual student or staff ID card to sign out materials
Young person's library card and Children's library card available (sub-pages)
what_you_need: Digital membership: join online instantly
Full membership: visit a library and show proof of name and address (driver's licence, passport, utility bill etc.)
Proof of name and address required when collecting a first reservation (upgrades you to full membership)
A standard reservation charge normally applies to reserved items

pages.title: Explore The Past
pages.summary: Explore the Past is home to the Worcestershire Archive and Archaeology Service: 12 miles of original archives, the Historic Environment Record, public research facilities on Level 2, plus professional archive/archaeology services; the dedicated explorethepast.co.uk website carries full service and opening-time details.
offerings: Explore the Past is home to the Worcestershire Archive and Archaeology Service and anything history related
Thousands of archaeological sites, historic buildings and landscapes are recorded and 12 miles of original archives are collected and preserved
An area for consulting original archives, up on level 2
Guidance and supervision from friendly, knowledgeable staff
Printed guidance and information to help you navigate indexes and collections
The facility to talk to archaeologists and access the archaeological record
A large self-service area for using microform and internet sources
A local studies and archaeology library
A conservation room for the repair and preservation of documents
Prearranged group visits 'behind the scenes'
A programme of history and archaeology based events and activities for all ages and interests
All types of research on Worcestershire people and places using the Historic Environment Record and the archive collections
Professional services: collect, preserve, make available and advise on archives
Professional services: undertake digitisation work on our own collections and those of others
Professional services: conserve documents in our care to ensure their long term preservation
Professional services: work with districts, county and regional and national agencies to manage and protect Worcestershire's Historic Environment
Professional services: deliver a wide range of professional archaeological services
Outreach & visits: tours, talks and school workshops
Newspaper microfilm collection temporarily unavailable (notice)

details.hours: See Explore The Past website for Archives and Archaeology opening times

pages.title: Book a space
pages.summary: Study spaces for students: over 800 study spaces across The Hive available for study, with Level 0 usable for group study.
offerings: There are over 800 study spaces across the Hive available to use for study
Level 0 can be used for group study

pages.title: Space for hire
pages.summary: Room hire at The Hive: affordable meeting, conference, event and training spaces from small huddle rooms to large function rooms, booked online with 360-degree virtual tours; video conferencing in many rooms; concessionary rates for not-for-profits; exhibition space also for hire.
offerings: From small huddle rooms to large function rooms, The Hive offers the perfect space with easily accessible, well-equipped rooms, at competitive prices
Affordable meeting space for meetings, conferences, events or training sessions
Wide range of spaces also available across Worcestershire Libraries
Online booking system with 360-degree virtual tour of Hive meeting rooms and facility checking (librarybookings.worcestershire.gov.uk)
University of Worcester members can continue to book Hive rooms for free for specific purposes
Concessionary rates available for not-for-profit organisations such as community groups, societies and charities
Video conferencing: new video-conferencing technology in many library meeting rooms (supported by the Library Improvement Fund and Arts Council England)
Exhibition space is available for hire - a variety of exhibition spaces, seeking ideas and activities to develop arts and cultural activities
Requests to hire other interior and exterior library space considered
A minimum one-hour charge applies to all bookings, with additional half hours available
All room booking prices include public liability insurance
Booking form and room layout documents available
what_you_need: Book online via librarybookings.worcestershire.gov.uk
When making a booking, you will be asked to sign and agree to the booking terms and conditions
Minimum one-hour charge applies to all bookings

details.prices: Competitive prices, updated; concessionary rates for not-for-profit organisations; minimum one-hour charge with additional half hours; public liability insurance included (specific figures not stated on page)

pages.title: Catalogue search
pages.summary: Gateway to three online catalogue searches: the library catalogue (quarter of a million titles plus all County branches), the archaeology report search via Heritage Gateway (HER), and the archives search via CalmView; also links to research databases and University of Worcester Library Services.
offerings: Library catalogue search: records for The Hive's quarter of a million titles plus simultaneous search of the stock of all County branches
Links to online resources, such as Ancestry and a range of research databases
University of Worcester Library Services: specialised services for University students and staff as well as the local community
Archaeology report search: Historic Environment Record (HER) information searchable online through the Heritage Gateway
Booking to see an HER officer at The Hive recommended for more detailed enquiries - they will explain the resources and conduct more detailed searches with you
Archives search: a significant proportion of the archive collections can be searched online (CalmView); paper catalogues at The Hive for the rest
what_you_need: University of Worcester students should log in to students.worc.ac.uk for the full range of educational resources

pages.title: Using the library
pages.summary: Explains the joint-library model: the first joint public and university library in Europe, with one user experience for everyone, university stock shelved alongside public stock, and integrated staff teams from the university, council libraries and council customer services.
offerings: The Hive is the first joint public and university library in Europe
Community members and university students access an outstanding facility with a vast variety of resources
Everyone gets the same user experience - from logging on to signing out books, whether student or member of the public
No segregation in the library: university stock is shelved alongside public stock
Staff from both the university and council work throughout the library; all users access the same services
Hive staff teams combine trained academic and public library staff with council customer service staff

pages.title: Special collections
pages.summary: Describes The Hive's special collections: the Stuart Collection (1,000+ books on Stuart-era Britain), the Local Studies and Archaeology libraries, the Palfrey Collection and the Stanbrook Abbey Press Collection, with consultation arrangements via the Explore the Past desk.
offerings: The Stuart Collection: over one-thousand books on the period of Stuart rule (1603-1714), largely bequeathed by John Grainger in 1900, including several hundred books printed during the period; many first and possibly last copies in existence
Browse Stuart Collection titles via the library catalogue using an advanced search for classification 'Stuart'
Local Studies library: one of the most extensive collections of books relating to Worcestershire's history and archaeology
Archaeology library: comprehensive collection covering all periods and most types of sites, particularly strong on the Home Front in the Second World War, with local, regional and national journals
The Palfrey Collection: rare antiquarian studies, first edition volumes and books crucial to the study of Worcestershire, bequeathed by Alderman H.E. Palfrey
The Stanbrook Abbey Collection: publications of the Stanbrook Abbey Press (founded 1876), on closed access on level 4 and catalogued
what_you_need: To consult Stuart Collection books, speak to a member of staff at the Explore the Past desk on Level 2
All viewings of fragile items must be performed in the original documents examination room within the 'access to original archives' opening hours

details.location_in_building: Explore the Past desk on Level 2; Stanbrook Abbey Collection on closed access on Level 4

pages.title: Research collections
pages.summary: Catalogue of the archive and local-history research collections available at The Hive (Level 2), from historic maps and the Historic Environment Record to census, probate, photographic, family, council and church records.
offerings: Staff available to assist in person during Explore The Past opening times - stop off at Level 2
Maps and plans, including historic Ordnance Survey, tithe and inclosure plans and estate plans; many available in digital formats for map comparison
The Historic Environment Record - a searchable database with digital mapping of all known archaeological sites, historic buildings and landscapes in the county

Sources for researching life stories, social history and your local area: Census returns, International Genealogical Index, General Register Office Index of Births, Marriages and Deaths, local newspapers, wills and other probate records
Photographs, including Worcestershire Photographic Survey and an Aerial photographic collection, most available digitally
Records of local families, groups, societies and businesses, including the archive of the Earls of Coventry of Croome
Quarter Sessions and County and District Council records including The Worcester City Archive
Records of the Diocese of Worcester as well as Church of England parishes and Non-conformist records
Local studies and archaeology library, including journals, trade and clerical directories
Special Collections also available

pages.title: Art collections
pages.summary: Describes the commissioned artworks in The Hive: Robert Orchardson's Kaleidoscope sculpture, Clare Woods' large-scale atrium painting Rack Alley, and two John Clare-inspired paintings by James Fisher.
offerings: Robert Orchardson, 'Kaleidoscope', aluminium, 2012 - commissioned sculpture designed to be visible from all three main floors of the building

Clare Woods, 'Rack Alley', 600x500cms, enamel and oil on aluminium, 2012 - made especially for the atrium of The Hive, commissioned by the University of Worcester and Worcestershire County Council to celebrate the opening
James Fisher, 'I Quaked Like the Ague' and 'Eat the Quids', oil on linen, 2008 - procured for The Hive by WLHC ProjectCo, inspired by poet John Clare's 1840 journey

pages.title: Careers Worcs Youth Hub
pages.summary: Careers Worcestershire @ The Hive is a dedicated space on Level 1 (Mon-Fri 9am-4pm) for young people aged 15-24, with careers advisors offering 1-1 support into employment, training and education, plus workshops and finance/health & wellbeing services.
offerings: Careers Worcs' Youth Hub is open Monday - Friday, 9am-4pm based on Level 1 in the Hive
Dedicated space for young people aged 15-24 to help find the right pathway into employment, training, and education
Dedicated careers advisors providing 1-1 support
Workshops and access to other services within the hub including finance, health & wellbeing, and other youth providers
Providing one to one advice and guidance sessions
Supporting you in finding, applying, and securing your next opportunity
Helping you find an apprenticeship or training programme
Confidence & resilience; personal development; career choices
CV writing; job search; job applications; interview preparation
Mental health/wellbeing support
Apprenticeships / Training courses / College & University courses
Work experience / Volunteering
Start your own business / Graduate jobs
Job search across the county via Worcestershire Jobs (worcestershirejobs.co.uk)
what_you_need: Aged 15-24 for the youth hub offer
Book an appointment via careersworcestershire.setmore.com

pages.title: Business Centre
pages.summary: The Business Centre at The Hive, located on Level 2, is home to the Business & IP Centre (BIPC) Worcestershire, providing free business advice, support, information and resources, plus a collection of technology-equipped meeting rooms.
offerings: The Hive is a valuable centre for businesses, as a provider of specialist resources and as a meeting and training venue
The Business Centre at The Hive is now home to the Business & IP Centre (BIPC) Worcestershire
The centre provides free and accessible business advice, support, information and resources
A collection of well-appointed meeting rooms, all equipped with the latest technology

pages.title: IT, Equipment & Printing
pages.summary: Level-by-level inventory of IT and AV equipment: catalogue search stations, public PCs with internet and Microsoft Office on every level, a 46-inch group study screen and two PS4 gaming zones on Level 0, printers/copiers and top-up kiosks throughout, scanners on Level 3 and privacy filters at the Level 1 Hub.
offerings: Dedicated stations to search the library catalogue, archive resources and archaeology resources
Level 0: Group study screen - HDMI cable to plug a laptop into a large 46" display for groups to share ideas
Level 0: Two gaming zones with two PS4 consoles
Level 0: Public access PCs with internet and software applications such as Microsoft Office
Level 0: High speed printer and a credit card top-up kiosk
Level 1 Children's library: public access PCs just for children; printer/copier
Level 1 Hub: high speed printer/copier and top-up kiosk - the only one in the building that takes cash
Level 2: public access PCs; the Explore the Past area has a high speed printer/copier
Level 3: public access PCs; A4 scanners available on request; two high speed printers/copiers and a credit card top-up kiosk
Level 4: public access PCs with internet and Microsoft Office
Privacy filters offered to all customers in the Hub area on level 1 - ask a member of staff
what_you_need: Read the Acceptable Use Agreement before using the computers at the Hive

pages.title: The Cafe at The Hive
pages.summary: The cafe serves hot and cold snacks, light meals and refreshments with a made-to-order deli bar, open 8.30am-9pm every day, with monthly offers, special-diet catering, a 10% student discount, five-star hygiene rating and full wheelchair accessibility; food supplied in partnership with Urban Eat.
offerings: A variety of hot and cold snacks, light meals and refreshments
Deli bar with fresh baguettes, sandwiches and salads, all prepared to order
Healthy choices such as yoghurt & muesli pots, plus indulgent treats
Monthly special offers and promotions
Special dietary requirements catered for: lactose- or gluten-free, vegetarian, vegan or other requirements
Student discount: 10% off food and drink purchased in the cafe (present student card at the till)
Catering for any meeting or function that takes place in The Hive
Five-star hygiene rating and commitment to customer service excellence
The cafe is fully accessible to wheelchair users, mobility scooters and pushchairs

Urban Eat ranges: CORE (incl. vegetarian, vegan, gluten free and halal), DELI (toasties, paninis, hot boxes), STREET (e.g. Bacon naan, Vegan Bean Burrito), ROOTS (Vegetarian Society approved vegan/vegetarian range)
what_you_need: Present your student card at the till to receive the 10% student discount
Food eaten in the cafe must be food which has been purchased from The cafe at The Hive

pages.title: Parking
pages.summary: Car and bicycle parking guidance: Cattlemarket pay-and-display directly in front (with 21 blue badge bays), Crowngate multi-storey opposite (with Shopmobility), Croft Road for long stay and high vehicles, two bicycle parking areas, and University Riverside car park arrangements for staff and students.
offerings: Cattlemarket pay and display car park directly in front of The Hive
21 designated blue badge disabled bays along the near side of The Hive, with ramped access to the main entrance
Crowngate Shopping Centre car park opposite The Hive - ticketed multi-storey; Shop mobility available on level six
Long stay and parking for high vehicles at Croft Road Car Park
Two bicycle park areas: one directly opposite the main entrance, one under the viaduct next to Cattlemarket car park

University of Worcester staff/students: student car parking permits valid at the University's Riverside car park (about 6 minutes' walk); University permits and St John's parking tickets are interchangeable at Riverside

pages.title: Transport
pages.summary: Public transport access: adjacent to Crowngate bus station, 4 minutes from Foregate Street train station, drop-off points on The Butts and Cattlemarket, walking route and bike share for university members, plus detailed last bus and last train timetables.
offerings: Adjacent to the main (Crowngate) bus station and a short walk (4 minutes) from Foregate Street train station
Foregate Street Train Station is 0.2 miles, 4 minutes, from The Hive, with stairs and an external access lift up to Sawmill Walk
Worcester Bus Station situated underneath Crowngate Shopping Centre directly opposite The Hive
Easy access drop off point on The Butts lay by, with public outside lift to Sawmill Walk; drop off also available in Cattlemarket car park
About 20 minutes' walk from St John's Campus for University students and staff
Bike share scheme: borrow a bike for 24 hours to ride between campuses; plenty of bike racks on the Hive site (worcester.ac.uk/bike)
The 31 bus stops on the St. John's Campus and runs right past The Hive
Last city buses, last other buses, last Foregate Street trains and last Shrub Hill trains timetables published on the page

pages.title: Find your space
pages.summary: Describes the variety of spaces across the five floors - for families, students and researchers - with purpose-designed furniture and layouts and sound dampening technology on all floors for both sociable and quiet use.
offerings: 5 floors with a range of different types of spaces, from families with children to students and researchers
Furniture, shelving and layouts designed to provide the best possible environment for learning and leisure
Wide open spaces and sheltered secluded study areas throughout
Sound dampening technology applied on all floors - noisy/chatty areas and solitude both available

pages.title: Physical accessibility
pages.summary: Comprehensive accessibility information: trained staff and enquiry points on every floor, bookable Meet & Greet, full wheelchair access with lifts and Braille signage, disabled toilets on every floor, a Level 1 personal care suite with shower and lifting facilities, adjustable workstations, induction loops, blue badge parking and pre-arranged close access.
offerings: Hive staff close to the main entrance, trained in all aspects of customer care
A call point near the entrance and an information / enquiry point on every floor
Arrange a Meet & Greet: staff will meet you, introduce the building, help you get around and assist with services
Fully accessible to wheelchair users, mobility scooters and pushchairs; ramp access to all areas on each floor
Lifts give access to all five public floors; clear signage with Braille underneath
Drinking water available on Levels 2 and 3 next to the lifts
Disabled toilet facilities on every floor, next to the lifts
Personal care suite on Level 1 with shower and lifting facilities (access cards from Hive staff, yours to keep for future visits)
Adjustable height working stations available throughout The Hive for computer use
Height adjustable tables for archive research and microfilm facilities in the Archive and Archaeology Service on Level 2
Automated microfilm machine with a foot pedal for customer use
Induction loops in Meeting rooms 2, 3 and The Studio (IR loop with headsets or neck loops available)
Selected information / enquiry points fitted with hearing loops (switch hearing aid to 'T' position)
Cattlemarket car park: 21 designated blue badge disabled bays, free for 3 hours
Crowngate Shop mobility available on level six (9:00am-5:30pm Mon-Sat, 10:30-4:00pm Sundays and Bank Holidays)
Easy access drop off point with lowered pavement on The Butts; public external lift to Sawmill Walk
Close access can be pre-arranged through Level 0 (call 01905 822866 or email hiveadminteam@worcestershire.gov.uk)
DisabledGo online accessibility checker for The Hive
what_you_need: Access card (from Hive staff on request) for the personal care suite
Pre-arrange Level 0 close access by phone or email in advance

details.location_in_building: Personal care suite on Level 1; height-adjustable archive tables on Level 2; drinking water Levels 2 and 3

pages.title: Inside the Hive
pages.summary: Floor-by-floor guide: Level 0 shared/group study with gaming and DVDs plus the secure archives; Level 1 children's library, cafe, exhibition spaces, The Studio theatre and Worcestershire Hub; Level 2 Explore the Past, meeting rooms and Business Centre; Level 3 the main integrated public and University library; Level 4 silent study; plus outdoor terraces, gardens, balconies and Story Island.
offerings: Level 0 - Shared study: social space with group study areas, gaming stations, comfortable chairs and vending machines; DVD and graphic novel collections; secure archives housed on Level 0 with occasional special tours

Level 1 - Discover: large and colourful children's library with access to 'Story Island', the cafe, exhibition spaces and The Studio theatre, The Worcestershire Hub, and a large selection of quick choice books

Level 2 - Explore the Past: archive and archaeology service for exploring the history of Worcestershire using original photos, maps, documents, archaeological evidence and digital resources; meeting rooms and a Business Centre with a suite of three rooms, an informal networking space and 24-hour newsfeed

Level 3 - Read, learn, imagine: main integrated public and University library with wide-ranging fiction, academic and general non-fiction; quiet study spaces, formal/informal reading places, comfortable chairs and sofas, views over the city, outdoor seating area
Level 4 - Research, quiet study: silent study floor with desks, chairs and window seating for peaceful reading and research
Rooms within The Hive can be used by customers to pray - ask a member of staff for an available private room (council policy equally supportive of all faiths)
Outdoors: landscaped environment with terraces and gardens, two balconies, and the unique 'Story Island' outdoor teaching space for children accessed through the children's library

pages.title: Food & drink
pages.summary: Food and drink rules: cafe on Level 1; visitors may bring their own cold food but no hot food; lids on hot drinks; eating and drinking allowed throughout the building except Level 2's Explore the Past section and Level 4 silent study.
offerings: The cafe on Level 1 offers a variety of food and drink
Visitors are welcome to bring their own cold food into the building, but no hot food is allowed
Users are asked to keep lids on their hot drinks
You can eat and drink in the cafe and throughout the building, except Level 2's Explore the Past section and Level 4 (the silent study area)

details.location_in_building: Cafe on Level 1; restrictions on Level 2 Explore the Past and Level 4

pages.title: School and College Visits
pages.summary: School and college visit packages for Early Years through Sixth Form & College: free 2-hour 'Package 1' visits (library tour plus curriculum activities) and charged 'Package 2' Explore the Past workshops from a minimum of £40 per hour/class; webinars and tailor-made packages also offered. More than 8,000 students have visited since 2012.
offerings: More than 8,000 students from schools and colleges have visited The Hive since it opened in 2012
Curriculum focused activities and workshops, building tours and library information sessions
Packages suitable for a whole class, or home educated groups, from age 5 up to secondary and sixth form
Early Years Package 1 (free, 2 hours): library tour, Storytime, How the library works, plus choice of Picture Book Races / Famous Author or Book Character Themed Visit / Christmas
Key Stage 1 Package 1 (free, 2 hours): library tour, Storytime, How the library works, plus choice of Picture Book Races / Book Sorter / Famous Author / Christmas
Key Stage 2 Package 1 (free, 2 hours): library tour, Storytime, How the library works tour, plus choice of Book Blitz / Book Sorter / Famous Author / Coding / Sustainability at The Hive / Christmas
Key Stage 3 Package 1 (free): library tour plus one of How the library works / Coding / Book Blitz / Sustainability at The Hive
Key Stage 4 Package 1 (free): library tour plus Research skills or Book speed dating

Sixth Form & College Package 1 (free): library tour plus one of Research skills / Using journals / Evaluating information for academic study / Referencing top tips (all referencing systems) / Introduction to Harvard Referencing / Spotting fake news

Package 2 (KS1 to Sixth Form): library tour plus choice of history, archive and archaeology workshops delivered by Worcestershire Archive and Archaeology Service (awarded Learning Outside the Classroom status in 2012) - minimum £40 per hour/class
Most sessions can be delivered to your classroom as a webinar; videos and worksheets available for some topics
Tailor-made packages themed to fit school work or national initiatives (Science Week, World Book Day, Time to Read, Summer Reading Challenge)
what_you_need: Package 2 costs a minimum of £40 per hour/class
Book via bookings@thehiveworcester.org

details.prices: Package 1 free; Package 2 minimum £40 per hour/class

pages.title: Children, young people & families
pages.summary: The Hive's children's library is one of the largest in the country, designed at children's scale with comfortable spaces for young children and carers, and featuring the Story Pit recessed seating area for readings, performances and group work.
offerings: The Hive's children's library is one of the largest in the country
Designed for children to find their own level and feel comfortable in spaces that are on their scale
Places for very young children to sit comfortably with their parents and carers; bright colours, easy-to-reach books, soft seating and interesting displays
Furniture and reading material adapts to its audience, providing something for all ages
The Story Pit: a recessed seating area in the heart of the library, perfect for readings, performances and group work

pages.title: Worcestershire Archive & Archaeology Service
pages.summary: Outlines WAAS's learning and outreach activities for participants from age 5 to retirees: research groups, talks, workshops, tours, school activities, community excavations, film screenings, family history fairs, guided walks and a geology trail; community project partnerships invited.
offerings: Local history research groups
Talks
Workshops
Tours
School activities
Community excavations
Archive film screenings
Family history fairs
Guided walks
Geology trail around The Hive (downloadable PDF)
Historical documents and artefacts used with groups to inspire learning and community projects
Open invitation to discuss collaborative community projects

pages.title: Students
pages.summary: Explains what The Hive offers University of Worcester students: four times the floor space of the old Peirson library, 250 bookable computers, printers/wifi/laptop points, access to council facilities including the records office and archaeology service, the dedicated history floor, the children's library for education students, and askalibrarian support.
offerings: Four times the floor space of the previous Peirson library - more spaces to study, relax and spend time
250 new computers, which you will be able to book in advance
Access to plenty of printers, wifi and laptop points
Access to a huge range of Council facilities, including the records office and archaeology service plus all of the public library books and other resources
Dedicated history floor with information on Worcestershire dating back to the last Ice Age - opportunity to undertake original research and develop high-level academic skills
The new children's library is one of the best in the country - valuable for teaching or early years qualifications and work experience opportunities
Library Services supports University of Worcester students, staff and local community in The Hive; askalibrarian team via email or lib chat
Student projects and Independent Studies supported when agreed with Hive staff in advance (guidance notes PDF)
what_you_need: Student projects and surveys must be discussed and agreed with Hive staff in advance

pages.title: Creating the Hive
pages.summary: History of the project: County Council and University first discussed a joint library in October 2004; Galliford Try appointed 2009; designed by Feilden Clegg Bradley Studios; built from 2010 on a derelict city-centre site; opened to the public 2 July 2012 and officially opened by Her Majesty the Queen on 11 July 2012; the name reflects purposeful activity and the golden honeycomb cladding.
offerings: A new multi-million pound city centre library, history and customer centre for students and the public
A combined facility for the whole community - a model unique and never undertaken before in this Country
Designed by Bath-based architects Feilden Clegg Bradley Studios; won the international Bentley Be Inspired Innovation in Generative Design award
Doors opened to the public on 2 July 2012; officially opened on 11 July 2012 by Her Majesty the Queen
Pedestrian route now links the cathedral, city centre, the library and the university's city campus

pages.title: Coronavirus information (10 December)
pages.summary: Legacy COVID-19 notice (dated 10 December): face coverings required unless exempt; customer number limits removed and seating reinstated; computers and desks available on demand without pre-booking; archive services fully open with no pre-booking; links to council and university COVID pages.
offerings: Public library open: customer numbers no longer limited, seating areas reinstated
Customers no longer have to pre-book computers or desk spaces - available on demand
Good ventilation of all public areas (including meeting rooms for hire), hand hygiene and surface cleaning maintained
Worcestershire Archive and Archaeology Service: archive services fully open; no pre-booking needed for search room spaces, archive self-service access or Historic Environment Records appointments
what_you_need: Anyone visiting a library will be required to wear a face covering at all times, unless medically exempt (per this dated notice)

pages.title: Jobs and volunteering
pages.summary: Employment and volunteering: full- and part-time jobs offered by either the University or the County Council (all staff work as one integrated service), with links to County Council, University and Bellrock vacancies; volunteer applications are currently paused due to unprecedented demand.
offerings: A range of exciting full- and part-time employment opportunities, offered either by the University or the County Council
County Council vacancies (link)
University of Worcester vacancies (link)
Careers at Bellrock (link)
Volunteering: a large and diverse team of volunteers - applications currently paused due to an unprecedented number of applications

pages.title: Awards
pages.summary: List of awards won by The Hive, spanning record-keeping, library design, sustainability, architecture and community impact from 2009 to 2017.
offerings: Record Keeping Service of the Year - ARA (Archives and Records Association) Excellence Awards, May 2017
Library Design Award (development over 2,000 square metres) - SCONUL Library Design Awards, December 2016
Service Provider of the Year - Worcester BID Customer Service Awards, April 2016
Outstanding Public Building - SCALA Civic Building of the Year Award, November 2013
Sustainability Award - British Construction Industry Awards, October 2013
Outstanding Library Team - Times Higher Leadership and Management Award, June 2013
National Award and Regional Award - RIBA, May 2013
Sustainability Award - RIBA Awards West Midlands, May 2013
Award for Design and Innovation - RICS Awards West Midlands, May 2013
Community Benefit Award - RICS Awards West Midlands, May 2013
Sustainable Project of the Year - Building Magazine Awards, April 2013
Civic Trust - Civic Trust Awards, March 2013
Contribution to the local community through The Hive - Guardian University Award, February 2013
New build project of the year (value above £5 million) - CIBSE Awards, February 2013
Building Excellence Award - South Worcestershire Building Control, 2012
Building of the year - Post Tensioned Society Awards, 2012
Outstanding Performance - BREEAM, June 2012
Best Sustainability in a Project - Public Private Partnership Awards, May 2012
West Midlands Regional Planning Award - Regional Planning Awards, 2012
Innovation in Generative Design - Bentley Be Inspired Awards, October 2009

pages.title: eBook library
pages.summary: BorrowBox eBook and eAudiobook service free to all library members 24/7: borrow up to six eBooks and six eAudiobooks at a time with automatic expiry (no late fees), via the website, library catalogue or BorrowBox app, syncing across up to six devices.
offerings: eBook and eAudiobook service provided by BorrowBox, available to all library members
Extensive range of best-selling fictional and non-fictional eBooks and eAudio titles, accessible 24/7
All titles in the collection are free to borrow
Up to six eBooks and six eAudiobooks can be borrowed at a time
Loans automatically expire at the end of the lending period, so no late fees
Access via the website (library.bolindadigital.com/worcestershire), via the library catalogue, or the BorrowBox app (Apple, Android, Kindle/Amazon Fire)
Borrowed titles synchronize across up to six devices on the same account
Web borrowing on PC/Mac uses Adobe Digital Editions; eAudiobooks loaned as protected MP3 files transferable to any device
Free classic eBooks signposted: Project Gutenberg, Manybooks.net, Feedbooks
University of Worcester students log in to students.worc.ac.uk for educational eBooks
what_you_need: Library membership; input your library card number and PIN
Join by visiting any Worcestershire library with proof of name and address, or join online instantly and upgrade later

pages.title: How to login to the public wi-fi
pages.summary: Page hosting an embedded instructional video (uniview.worc.ac.uk) showing how to log in to The Hive's public wi-fi; no further text content.
offerings: Public wi-fi available at The Hive, with an embedded how-to-login video guide

pages.title: Research databases
pages.summary: The Hive provides walk-in access to ~37 research databases for all library members inside the building (not at home or other County libraries), covering millions of journal and newspaper articles, conference papers and original documents, with staff help available.
offerings: Research databases available on a walk-in basis to all library members within the building itself, but not at home or in other County libraries
Staff in the Hive will demonstrate the best methods for searching these resources and advise on the best database for your needs
Academic Search Complete (multi-disciplinary, nearly 8,500 full-text periodicals)
Access to Research
ACLS Humanities E-Book Collection
ACM Digital Library
Ancestry.com - Library Edition (1 billion searchable UK family history records)
Archaeology Data Service
BMJ Journals
British Education Index
British Periodicals Online
Business Source Complete
Cambridge University Press Journals (Cambridge Core)
Cambridge Companions to Literature and the Classics
CINAHL Complete
BIPC Business database reference resources (on the 'Business Research Databases' in The Hive Business Centre on level 2, courtesy of The Hive partnership with Worcestershire County Council)
Dictionary of National Biography (log in with WOR + library number)
Drama online (full-text plays)
Early Modern Books
ERIC
Gale Primary Sources: British newspapers
Google Scholar
House of Commons Parliamentary Papers - 18th, 19th and 20th Century
Internet Archaeology Archive
JSTOR
Jane Austen's Manuscripts
Oxford English Dictionary (log in with WOR + library number)
Oxford Reference Online - Premium Collection (over 2 million entries)
Oxford University Press Journals (over 330 journals)
Project Gutenberg
Project Muse: Humanities
ProQuest Archives
PsycARTICLES
Science Direct Open Access Journals and Science Direct Journals
SAGE Premier
Social Care Online
SPORTDiscus with full text
Taylor & Francis Full Collection
Times Digital Archive
Research database PDF guide downloadable
what_you_need: Library membership; access from within the building only (walk-in)
Resources marked with an asterisk are for personal educational use only; copying limited by Copyright Law (one article per periodical issue, 5% or one chapter of a book)
University members log in to student or staff pages as normal for the full range

details.location_in_building: BIPC business databases on the Business Research Databases terminals in The Hive Business Centre on level 2

pages.title: Young person's library card
pages.summary: A dedicated 'street art' library card for Worcestershire teenagers aged 13-19, designed by local artist Louis Burrows - believed to be the first dedicated teen library card in the Midlands - with instant-membership rules for ages 8-16.
offerings: A funky 'street art' library card for Worcestershire's teenagers aged 13 - 19 years, designed by local artist Louis Burrows
Believed to be the first dedicated library card for teenagers in the Midlands
Join at your local library or via the Online Library Hub for online services and the eLibrary
Teenagers aged 8 - 16 years can become instant members, entitling them to borrow up to two books without a parent or guardian signature
Instant membership upgradeable to full membership by visiting the library with a parent or guardian with ID showing name and address
what_you_need: For full membership upgrade: visit with a parent or guardian and identification showing name and address
Parent/guardian visit required to add computer access and book borrowing to online-only membership

pages.title: Children's library card
pages.summary: Worcestershire's children's library card, designed by Tom Gates author Liz Pichon; full membership for children requires a parent or guardian's signature, while ages 8-16 can become instant members and borrow up to 2 books.
offerings: Children's library card designed by Liz Pichon, author of the Tom Gates book series, featuring characters from the books
Children and teenagers aged 8 - 16 years can become instant members at The Hive, entitling them to borrow up to 2 books without a parent/guarantor signature
Child instant membership upgradeable to full membership by visiting with a parent or guardian and ID showing name and address
what_you_need: Children need a parent or guardian's signature to join the library with full membership

pages.title: Catering
pages.summary: Hospitality catering for meetings, corporate training and conferences at The Hive, delivered by the onsite catering company that also runs the cafe: buffets, platters, canapes, business breakfasts and special-diet options, booked with at least 2 working days' notice.
offerings: The Hive has its own onsite catering company, who also manage our excellent cafe on level 1
Hospitality menu with a variety of delicious buffets and platters, fresh fruit, cake and breakfast options
Side dishes such as savoury mini quiches
Special diets catered for, including dairy-free, gluten-free, vegetarian and vegan
Canapes and finger food for meetings requiring 'just a nibble'
Business breakfasts for early meetings
Hospitality booking form downloadable
what_you_need: Download the hospitality booking form and email it to hivecatering@bellrockgroup.co.uk, including your meeting room booking reference and meeting room number
Minimum of 2 working days' notice to add catering to a room booking; final numbers confirmed by that time; later cancellations/changes subject to additional charges

pages.title: Supporting business
pages.summary: The Archive and Archaeology Service at The Hive advises landowners, developers and consultants on archaeological implications and opportunities of development in the County, advises on historic environment management and conservation, and runs heritage workshops and training.
offerings: Advice to landowners, developers and consultants on the archaeological implications and potential opportunities of development in the County
Early conversations to clarify the process and keep archaeology-related costs to a minimum
Advice on the management and conservation of the rural and urban historic environment
Input into community-focused projects and HLF (Heritage Lottery Fund) projects
Workshops and training sessions on a wide variety of heritage and historic environment topics

details.contact: Historic Environment Policy and Advisory Manager Emma Hancox ehancox@worcestershire.gov.uk; Historic Environment Advisor Adam Mindykowski amindykowski@worcestershire.gov.uk

pages.title: Research resources (Business)
pages.summary: Business and IP Centre resources: free access to the COBRA, Statista and FAME business databases from any public computer in The Hive, plus walk-in research databases and signposting to BIPC Worcestershire for start-up support, IP information and funding.
offerings: The Business and IP Centre at The Hive provides free access to a selection of business databases and up to date business information
COBRA (Complete Business Reference Adviser) - free from any public computer in The Hive
Statista - free from any public computer in The Hive
FAME - free from any public computer in The Hive
Access to many other research databases on a walk-in basis for all library members within the building
Business information including start-up support, Intellectual Property information, and funding via the BIPC Worcestershire website
what_you_need: Walk-in databases available to library members within the building only

pages.title: Catering (legacy page)
pages.summary: Older duplicate of the catering page (linked under the cafe section) with the same hospitality offering - buffets, platters, canapes, business breakfasts, special diets - using earlier 2021 menu and bellrock.fm contact addresses.
offerings: The Hive has its own onsite catering company, who also manage our excellent cafe on level 1
Hospitality menu (2021 version) with buffets, platters, fresh fruit, cake and breakfast options
Special diets catered for, including dairy-free, gluten-free, vegetarian and vegan
Canapes and finger food; business breakfasts
what_you_need: Download the hospitality booking form and email to hivecatering@bellrock.fm with meeting room booking reference and room number
Minimum of 2 working days' notice; final numbers required by then; later changes subject to additional charges

pages.title: Activities & workshops - descriptions
pages.summary: Full descriptions of school visit activities (story time, library skills, Book Blitz, coding with Scratch, research skills, journals and more) and the chargeable Explore the Past history/archaeology workshops (around £80, 90-120 minutes) covering prehistory to WWII, including joint workshops with other Worcester heritage venues.
offerings: Story time: fun, interactive story time linked to the curriculum or a topic of your choice
How the library works - finding what you need: finding your way around a school or public library and using the library catalogue (themable to your topic)
Book blitz: lively 45-60 minute session encouraging a wider range of books, authors and genres (Years 4 upwards)
Picture Book Races: engaging children in reading for pleasure using picture book covers (Years R-3)

Famous Author / Book Character Themed Visit: workshops themed around famous authors or book characters (e.g. Harry Potter, Roald Dahl); chargeable add-on activity on request at £2.50 / child (e.g. BFG dream jars)
Book Sorter: pupils create a reading list for their class in a teamwork exercise (Years 3 upwards)
Coding: 1 hour introduction to computer coding using Scratch to create an animation or game (Years 4-7)
Sustainability at The Hive: workshop celebrating the building's energy efficiency and sustainability footprint (Years 4 upwards)
Christmas: festive stories and Christmas card making; cards distributed to housebound customers (Years R-6)

Research skills: minimum 2.5 hours, general or subject specific; university-library experience, finding resources, keywords, evaluating online resources; extendable to a full day (10am-3pm) with poster project assessment - beneficial for HPQ or EPQ students
Using journals: one hour introduction to academic journals and online databases

Explore the Past workshops by Worcestershire Archive & Archaeology Service: most last around 90-120 minutes and cost around £80; primarily KS2 but adaptable from Year 1 to sixth form; can be delivered in schools and colleges
Prehistoric Worcestershire workshop (can include Iron Age village clay workshop at Worcester City Museum & Art Gallery)
Romans in Worcester workshop (The Hive is built on the site of a busy part of Roman Worcester)
Roman Kitchen & Cooking workshop
Anglo-Saxons (Invaders and Settlers) workshop with replica skeleton and grave goods
Tudor Worcester workshop using documents and archaeological evidence
Civil War in Worcester workshop (combinable with Worcester Cathedral tower trip or Commandery workshop)
Victorian Worcester - Public Health: joint two-site visit with The Infirmary medical museum
What was it like to live here? Victorian Worcester / The Lives of the Poor workshops
World War I resource-pack-based visits (war memorials/tracing soldiers, life in Worcestershire, letters from the front)
Archaeology of World War II workshop, with optional second session at Ronkswood Meadows searchlight bases
Local History Study advice on maps, photos and census material
Teacher advice on running local study projects using The Hive's holdings

details.prices: Famous Author add-on activity £2.50 per child; Explore the Past workshops around £80 (90-120 minutes)

pages.title: Resource packs
pages.summary: Five WAAS classroom resource packs containing copies of documents from The Hive's collections with teaching notes: Civil War and 'What was it like to live here in the Past?' at £20 each, plus three WW1 packs available free from the Worcestershire WW100 project website.
offerings: Five resource packs developed by Worcestershire Archive and Archaeology Service for use in the classroom, containing copies of documents from the collections at The Hive together with notes
Worcestershire in the Civil War - £20
What was it like to live here in the Past? - £20
World War 1 - War Memorials (free download from ww1worcestershire.co.uk)
World War 1 - Worcestershire during WW1 (coming soon)
World War 1 - Letters from the Front (coming soon)

details.prices: £20 each for Civil War and 'What was it like to live here' packs; WW1 packs free to download

pages.title: Booking your visit
pages.summary: How to book a school/college visit: contact bookings@thehiveworcester.org (or explorethepast@worcestershire.gov.uk / 01905 766352 for Explore the Past-only workshops); The Hive is also available as a venue and resource for teacher training days.
offerings: School and college visit bookings via bookings@thehiveworcester.org
Explore the Past workshop-only bookings via explorethepast@worcestershire.gov.uk or 01905 766352
The Hive available as a venue and resource for teacher training days
Workshops for teachers on using libraries to encourage independent learning and on The Hive's resources for teachers and students
what_you_need: Inform The Hive of any special requirements for children or accompanying adults when booking
The safety of school and college visitors remains the responsibility of the teacher throughout (The Hive conforms to its own safeguarding policy)

pages.title: Joining the library (schools)
pages.summary: Library membership for school visitors: children can join during a visit and borrow books; under-16 full membership needs an adult guarantor; automatic membership available if the school supplies names and dates of birth; instant membership (age 8+) allows 2 books; Schools' Bulk Loan Library Card available via the Worcestershire Schools' Library Service.
offerings: Children and students who are not already library members can join during a school or college visit and borrow books to take home
The library card is valid in all Worcestershire public libraries - books borrowed from The Hive can be returned to the nearest Worcestershire library
Automatic library membership available by providing a full list of first names, surnames and dates of birth for visiting students
Instant membership for students aged 8 plus: borrow up to two books without a parent/guarantor signature, with an explanatory letter sent home
School's Bulk Loan Library Card - Worcestershire Schools' Library Service offers schools and Home Educators a wide range of up-to-date resources and reading material (contact SLS on 01905 420273)
what_you_need: Full membership for children under 16 requires an adult guarantor signature; adult permission also required for child computer use
Instant membership (8-16) upgradeable by visiting with a parent/guardian and ID showing name and address; 16+ upgrade with own proof of identity

pages.title: Visit information (schools)
pages.summary: Practical information for school visits: travel and parking directions, coach drop-off on The Butts, non-secured bag/coat storage in the Level 1 children's library, bookable lunch rooms (hire charge) or packed-lunch spaces, pre-orderable cafe food, and toilets plus a personal care suite.
offerings: Train directions (Worcester Foregate Street) and step-free access route
Coach or minibus drop-off point on The Butts; nearest car park for minibuses and larger vehicles is Croft Road
Parking on the Crowngate car park and Cattle Market car park with safe pedestrian access
Bag and coat storage: a non-secured area on level 1 in the children's library
Rooms available to book in advance for lunch (hire charge applies); spaces can be made available for students to eat packed lunches
Food can be purchased from the cafe and pre-booked by emailing hivecatering@bellrock.fm at least one day in advance
Cafe menu downloadable (PDF)
Toilets, including disabled facilities, on every floor; personal care suite on level 1
what_you_need: Advise at booking if packed-lunch space is required
Pre-order cafe food at least one day in advance via hivecatering@bellrock.fm
Hire charge applies to rooms booked for lunch

details.location_in_building: Bag/coat storage and personal care suite on Level 1

pages.title: Facts and figures
pages.summary: Headline statistics for The Hive: opened July 2012, first of its kind in Europe, 12 miles of archives, a quarter of a million books, 10,000m2 of public space over five floors, 800 study stations and over 26,000 historic monument records.
offerings: Opened July 2012
Is the first of its kind in Europe
Houses 12 miles of archive collections
Winner of two international awards for the building's design and sustainability
Contains a quarter of a million books
Anticipating over a million visitors per year
Over a million books issued in the building's first year of opening
10,000m2 of public space, over five floors
800 study stations
Over 26,000 records of historic monuments and buildings
Landmark, distinctive design

pages.title: The building
pages.summary: Describes the building's design and environment: colour/lighting/acoustic zoning, renewable energy and recycling, natural light and ventilation, sustainable and recyclable materials, grey water recycling, green travel facilities, and radiant heating/cooling from concrete ceilings with automatic windows.
offerings: Each area within The Hive is defined by colour, lighting and acoustics to create distinct and appropriate zones
The building uses renewable energy and has exemplar recycling facilities throughout
Maximum use of controlled natural light and ventilation, with local manual override for user comfort
Water use minimised by water saving fittings and the recycling of grey water
Green travel facilitated via cycle parking, staff showers and excellent public transport connections
Heating and cooling controlled automatically; majority delivered by radiant effect from concrete ceilings with embedded pipework; majority of the building naturally ventilated by automatic windows

pages.title: Sustainability
pages.summary: Details the sustainability systems: natural ventilation and summertime cooling, daylight strategy, biomass heating, river water cooling using the River Severn as a heat sink, rainwater harvesting for WC flushing and archaeological washing, water meadows and 4000m2+ of biodiversity planting; the building has beaten its energy targets.
offerings: Natural ventilation & summertime cooling strategy: perimeter opening windows, atrium stack ventilation, below-ground supply duct, evaporative cooling over the water meadow, exposed concrete thermal mass with night-time pre-cooling, river-fed chilled beams/slab cooling in very hot weather
Daylight strategy: design maximises daylight to cut the ~30% of building energy normally used for artificial lighting
Renewable energy strategy: biomass boilers burning organic material (considered carbon neutral)
River water cooling: River Severn used as a heat sink via heat exchanger, returned only a few degrees warmer, hydraulically separate from building water
Water management: rainwater harvesting serves WC flushing and archaeological washing requirements

Planting & urban ecology: two water meadows for flood attenuation planted with native wildflowers including Worcestershire's county flower, the cowslip; over 4000m2 of soft landscape dedicated to biodiversity; bird nesting, bat roosting and stag beetle hibernacula features
Monitoring: The Hive has comfortably beaten its energy targets (CIBSE Journal case study linked)

note: Built page-by-page from thehiveworcester.org. Every fact carries its source page. Where the Hive site and worcestershire.gov.uk conflict (hours, prices, membership), the council page wins; the site itself flags whats-on.html as static/under-construction.
